questions.question: What is the primary function of a clock signal in synchronous digital circuits?
options: To regulate the power supply voltage to all components.
To provide a means for storing data in combinational logic.
To synchronize the operations of different components and sequential logic elements.
To serve as the primary input for all arithmetic operations.
To dissipate excess heat generated by the circuit.

questions.question: What does Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) primarily define in computer systems?
options: The physical layout and manufacturing process of the CPU.
The voltage levels and power consumption characteristics of the processor.
The micro-architectural details and specific hardware implementation of the CPU.
The abstract interface between software and hardware, including instruction formats and registers.
The operating system's kernel structure and memory management policies.

questions.question: In the physical verification phase of Integrated Circuit (IC) design, what is the primary purpose of Design Rule Checking (DRC)?
options: To verify the logical correctness of the RTL code against the specification.
To optimize the power consumption of the entire chip.
To ensure that the physical layout adheres to manufacturing process constraints and electrical rules.
To simulate the dynamic behavior of the circuit under various input stimuli.
To generate automatic test patterns for production testing.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of Static Timing Analysis (STA) in the digital IC design flow?
options: To dynamically simulate the circuit's behavior at specific clock frequencies.
To identify and fix functional bugs in the RTL design.
To verify that the circuit meets all specified timing constraints (e.g., setup and hold times) without requiring test vectors.
To optimize the overall area consumption of the layout.
To generate a power report detailing average and peak power usage.

questions.question: In modern computer architectures, what is the primary purpose of 'virtual memory'?
options: To increase the processing speed of the Central Processing Unit (CPU) by directly accelerating arithmetic operations.
To reduce the physical size and power consumption of the main memory modules.
To provide an illusion of a larger main memory than physically exists and to offer memory protection among processes.
To directly speed up Input/Output (I/O) operations by bypassing the CPU.
To simplify the instruction decoding logic within the processor pipeline.
